channel.title: MCP News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated news about Model Context Protocol, AI agents, tool integrations, MCP security, and developer tooling.

item.title: The Silent Killer of Context Windows: Why Token Estimation is Failing Your Agents
item.description: If you are building LLM-powered agents, you have likely run into the 'context wall.' You send a massive payload of documentation or history to Claude or GPT-4o, and suddenly the model starts hallucinating, truncating mid-sentence, or—even worse—throwing an API error because you exceeded the window....
item.dc:creator: Renato Marinho

item.title: The settlement primitive no agent tool ships yet: an atomic forward (settle at T+24h, never custody the gap)
item.description: Almost every settlement tool an AI agent can reach today does one thing: it clears the trade right now, in the next block or two. Spot swap, spot escrow, spot payment. That covers a lot of ground. It does not cover the trade that is agreed today and settled tomorrow. That trade has a name in...
item.dc:creator: Baris Sozen

item.title: AI Agent Tool Sprawl: How Anthropic's 2026 Upgrades Fix It
item.description: The hidden cost of connecting AI agents to more systems Most businesses that adopt AI agents start small: one agent watching a WhatsApp inbox, or one agent pulling leads into a CRM. Then it works, and the natural next step is to connect that agent to more systems: inventory, calendar, billing, a...
item.dc:creator: Abhijeet Singh

item.title: Amazon Seller Competitor Research Methods: A Developer's Guide with Code
item.description: Author: Leo, Technical Lead at Pangolinfo Tags: amazon python api mcp web-scraping data-analysis Reading time: ~12 minutes TL;DR This tutorial walks through building a complete Amazon competitor research system using Python and the Pangolinfo API. You'll learn the 5-step IBADM framework (Identify →...
item.dc:creator: Pangolinfo

item.title: MCP Series (05): Resources and Prompts Deep Dive — Dynamic Data, Parameterized URIs, and Multi-Turn Templates
item.description: Resources vs Tools The split: Tools → actions the LLM executes (verbs) LLM decides when to call; calls may have side effects Examples: create_issue, update_status Resources → data the LLM reads (nouns) Host decides when to inject; read-only, no side effects Examples: current Sprint status, project...
item.dc:creator: WonderLab

item.title: Zero to MCP Server in 30 Minutes
item.description: What Is MCP? MCP (Model Context Protocol) is becoming the standard way for AI applications (Claude, Cursor, VS Code, etc.) to discover and call external tools. Think of it as “USB‑C for AI.” One MCP server → works with every MCP‑compatible client. Build a SERP Search MCP Server Here’s a complete,...
item.dc:creator: talor

item.title: QR Code Generator API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기
item.description: QR Code Generator API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기 이커머스, 출석체크, 메뉴판, 결제링크 — QR 필요한 곳 어디든 프로젝트에 qr code generator 기능이 필요한데 직접 구현하기엔 시간이 아까운가요? REST API 한 줄이면 됩니다. Quick Start # 즉시 테스트 (API 키 불필요) curl https://api.lazy-mac.com/qr-code/ 실전 예제 Python import requests resp =...
item.dc:creator: 2x lazymac

item.title: I created a protocol for AI agents to talk to each other — ACP (Agent Communication Protocol)
item.description: The problem AI agents are getting powerful. Claude can write code. Cursor can edit files. AutoGen can orchestrate multi-agent workflows. CrewAI can run crews of agents. But agents can't find each other. If I'm an agent that can analyze data, and I need an agent that can scrape websites — how do I...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: I built a protocol for AI agents to discover, evaluate, and install MCP skills autonomously
item.description: The problem AI agents (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline) can install MCP servers to extend their capabilities. But there's no standard way for agents to: Discover which MCP servers exist Evaluate if a server is safe to install Purchase paid skills autonomously Verify the install succeeded Recover from...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: The Best Stock Market APIs in 2026: A Hedge Fund Manager’s Tier List
item.description: After years of managing capital and evaluating financial-data infrastructure, I have learned that the most dangerous data problems are rarely obvious. A plausible but incorrect price can quietly contaminate a backtest, distort a risk model, or generate a trade that should never have existed. The...
item.dc:creator: Joseph Rolet | Hedge Fund

item.title: MCP-Ready MVPs: Should Startups Build Tool-Connected AI Products From Day One?
item.description: A normal AI feature generates an answer. A tool-connected AI product can do something. It can search a company database, create a support ticket, update a project, retrieve a customer record, schedule a meeting, or trigger an internal workflow. This shift from answering to acting is one of the most...
item.dc:creator: Nasif Sid

item.title: Build a Read-Only Analytics Tool Before Giving Your Agent Actions
item.description: The fastest way to make an analytics agent impressive is to let it answer broad natural-language questions. The fastest way to make it dangerous is to give the same path export, mutation, or deployment authority. Ship one narrow read-only slice first. type MetricRequest = { tenantId: string;...
item.dc:creator: kongkong

item.title: Agent Runs now available in the Vercel MCP and CLI
item.description: Your agent can now inspect Agent Runs via the Vercel MCP and CLI for eve, the open-source agent framework.eve traces are automatically ingested when deployed to Vercel and available as Agent Runs. The new Vercel MCP tools and Vercel CLI commands let you find projects with runs, list recent runs,...
item.dc:creator: Brooke Mosby

item.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
item.description: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
item.dc:creator: Will Papper

item.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
item.description: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol

item.title: MCP for Beginners: Why Every AI Engineer and Developer Should Learn the Model Context Protocol
item.description: If you have spent any time building with large language models in the last year, you have hit the same wall everyone hits: your model is brilliant at reasoning but blind to the real world. It cannot read your database, call your internal API, search your documents, or trigger a deployment unless...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
item.description: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. As soon as an MCP server does anything meaningful, the same questions that govern any API...
item.dc:creator: vzisiadis

item.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
item.description: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol
